fixes_applied: Replaced auto-generated SWE-rebench fallback test.sh (which gave ~0.9948 unconditionally because FAIL_TO_PASS is empty and overall vitest pass-rate ~573/576 is reached on base) with a structural Python-regex verifier driven from manager.ts source. Verifier is self-contained (no typescript-package dependency) and implements the four F2P gates already declared in test_manifest.yaml.

Bypassed the existing tests/verify_fix.ts which crashes under typescript@6.x (which bun caches globally) — it uses node.name?.getText(source) and TS6 tightened that signature. The previous test.sh never invoked it anyway.
f2p_added: dragleave_fix (weight 0.30) — setupDropZone's dragleave handler uses getBoundingClientRect + clientX/clientY + rect.{left,right,top,bottom} boundary check, OR the classList.remove(...gv-folder-dragover...) call is gated inside a nested block (not at the top level of the handler body). Implementation-agnostic: accepts < or <=, accepts e.relatedTarget-style alternatives if they keep the remove call gated.

ensure_method (weight 0.25) — A private helper method exists with (folderId, dragData) parameters whose body matches the post-fix signature: writes to this.data.folderContents[<id>], handles BOTH dragData.conversations (multi-select) AND dragData.conversationId (single), is silent (no removeConversationFromFolder/saveData/refresh calls). Distinct from the pre-existing addConversationToFolder which only handles a single conversation and calls removeConversationFromFolder. Method NAME is not enforced — any private name passes.

drop_preinsert (weight 0.25) — Some `if (!sourceFolderId)` guard calls the helper method, and that guard precedes a reorderOrMoveConversations(...) call within ~1500 chars (i.e. inside the same drop-handler region).
method_depth (weight 0.20) — The helper body has folderContents access + iteration (for/forEach/map/reduce) + push/spread + sortIndex/maxSortIndex + .some() existence check + ≥4 statements.
notes: Triple-check overturns DROP. Three converging pathologies on the prior task: (1) the auto-generated SWE-rebench fallback test.sh runs `bun x vitest run` over the entire repo with empty FAIL_TO_PASS, so it falls back to overall pass-rate (573/576 = 0.9948) regardless of whether the fix is applied — all five trials across cohorts scored exactly 0.9948, identical to what a no-op agent would score. (2) The tests/ directory already contained a thoughtful test_manifest.yaml with 4 F2P gates AND a custom verify_fix.ts AST checker that the test.sh never invoked. (3) The verify_fix.ts itself crashes under typescript@6.x (which bun caches globally) because it uses `node.name?.getText(source)` and TS6 made that signature stricter. The new test.sh implements the same four conceptual gates using pure Python regex/brace-matching against manager.ts source, eliminating the TS dependency. SMOKING-GUN VALIDATION PERFORMED: cloned Nagi-ovo/gemini-voyager at base commit 1f42255 and patched commit cb2f2441, ran the new test.sh against both. Base scored 0.0000 (all 4 gates fail). Patched scored 1.0000 (all 4 gates pass). Two alternative-implementation variants (helper renamed to `insertConversationsIntoFolder`; strict `<` instead of `<=` in coordinate check) both scored 1.0000 — confirming the verifier is implementation-agnostic. A partial fix that only touches dragleave (the easy half) scored 0.30, giving correct partial credit. Original prior-DROP concern about over-fitting addressed: helper-method NAME is not enforced (only the post-fix signature shape is), and dragleave gate accepts either coordinate-check OR conditional-remove patterns. The original session has 4 user messages over ~10.5 hours with a long silence then a 'why?' nudge then a follow-up reporting a second positioning issue — exactly the iterative-correction signal this benchmark is designed to measure.
